judgment.summary: Intel shows strong revenue growth and a significant EPS beat, but suffers from negative profit margins and free cash flow, alongside high forward P/E and analyst skepticism.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat consensus by a wide margin; next earnings date is 2026-07-23.
positives: Revenue growth 7.2% YoY indicates top-line expansion.
Last quarter EPS beat estimate by 2108.7% (0.29 vs 0.01).
Analyst growth estimates are very high for the current quarter (311.3%) and fiscal year (159.1%).
Strong cash position of $32.79B and healthy current (2.31) and quick (1.66) ratios.
negatives: Profit margin is negative at -5.9%, and Return on Equity is -2.9%.
Free cash flow is negative at $-8.30B TTM.
Forward P/E of 76.96x is very high, suggesting high growth expectations are priced in.
Mean analyst target of $87.86 is significantly below the current stock price, with a consensus 'hold' rating.

news.paragraph: Intel is seeing mixed signals, with a prominent media personality calling it one of the greatest turnarounds he's ever seen, while broader market headlines discuss the impact of AI on dividends and potential government investment in quantum computing firms. The company recently reported a significant EPS beat for its last quarter, and the next earnings date is set for July 23rd. Traders should consider the divergence between the bullish commentary and the broader sector trends impacting dividend payouts.

headlines.title: The Trump Administration Plans to Take Stakes in These Quantum Computing Firms. Their Stocks Are Soaring.
